data.title: Three members of the Federal Reserve may want to keep interest rates unchanged at this meeting
data.description: According to IFR, a Reuters affiliate, the first headline for market participants, aside from the 25 basis point rate cut, is likely to be the dissenting opinion of three or more officials. Waller, Bowman, and Milan will undoubtedly be the three who favor a larger rate cut, while the other dissenters could be Goolsbee, Schmid, or Mousalem, who favor holding rates steady. However, what traders are really focused on is the FOMC's dot plot, predicting only one rate cut in 2025 or perhaps two more. Trump's strategy in removing Fed Governor Cook is likely intended to ensure two more rate cuts in 2025 after today's cut. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump's second son: Cryptocurrency is the "perfect hedge tool" for real estate assets
data.description: Eric Trump, the second son of Trump, said that cryptocurrency is the "perfect hedge tool" for real estate assets, and the Trump Organization has "almost no debt" and is committed to owning a share of Bitcoin in the United States. (Jinshi)

data.title: BlackRock's $185 billion portfolio is undergoing a major rebalance, with bets on AI and US stocks.
data.description: An investment outlook report indicates that BlackRock (BLK.N), the world's largest asset manager, is "upgrading its risk allocation" by significantly increasing its holdings in US stocks and increasing its exposure to artificial intelligence (AI) across its $185 billion model portfolio platform. The outlook report notes that, thanks to the US stock market's "top-tier earnings performance," BlackRock increased its allocation to US stocks across its model portfolios at the expense of international developed market stocks. Following the adjustments, the portfolios' overall equity holdings were 2% overweight. Data shows that on Tuesday, as BlackRock completed its asset allocation adjustments, billions of dollars in flows occurred between its corresponding exchange-traded funds (ETFs). BlackRock's adjustments to its model portfolios are a vote of confidence in the US stock market's rally: the S&P 500 has reached record highs this year, driven by a surge in AI investment and market bets that the Federal Reserve is about to embark on a cycle of interest rate cuts. BlackRock said in an investment report that relatively strong earnings performance by U.S. companies will drive U.S. stocks higher, noting that since the third quarter of 2024, U.S. corporate earnings have grown by 11%, while earnings growth for similar companies in other developed markets has been less than 2%.

data.title: Curve Finance founder proposes the launch of the Yield Basis protocol to distribute sustainable returns to CRV holders
data.description: Curve Finance founder Michael Egorov announced a proposal on the Curve DAO governance forum, proposing the establishment of a protocol called Yield Basis to create three Bitcoin-focused pools designed to distribute sustainable returns to CRV holders. Holders stake their tokens to participate in governance votes and receive veCRV tokens in return. According to the proposal, Curve will mint $60 million worth of crvUSD stablecoins before the launch of Yield Basis. The proceeds will be used to support three Bitcoin-focused pools: WBTC, cbBTC, and tBTC, each capped at $10 million. (CoinDesk)
